  • Core models

Core models#

Most users should use the high-level sqd.tl API. The core model classes are documented for users who need to extend scQDiff or integrate it into custom training pipelines.

class scqdiff.DriftConfig(dim: 'int' = 64, hidden: 'int' = 256, depth: 'int' = 4, beta: 'float' = 0.1, use_spectral_norm: 'bool' = True, use_velocity_prior: 'bool' = False, vel_scale: 'float' = 2.0, vel_k: 'int' = 15, vel_tau: 'float' = 1.0, vel_time_mode: 'str' = 'flat', vel_conf_power: 'float' = 1.0, alpha_control: 'float' = 0.001, jacobian_dim_warn: 'int' = 500)[source]#

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • dim (int)

  • hidden (int)

  • depth (int)

  • beta (float)

  • use_spectral_norm (bool)

  • use_velocity_prior (bool)

  • vel_scale (float)

  • vel_k (int)

  • vel_tau (float)

  • vel_time_mode (str)

  • vel_conf_power (float)

  • alpha_control (float)

  • jacobian_dim_warn (int)

alpha_control: float = 0.001#
beta: float = 0.1#
depth: int = 4#
dim: int = 64#
hidden: int = 256#
jacobian_dim_warn: int = 500#
use_spectral_norm: bool = True#
use_velocity_prior: bool = False#
vel_conf_power: float = 1.0#
vel_k: int = 15#
vel_scale: float = 2.0#
vel_tau: float = 1.0#
vel_time_mode: str = 'flat'#
class scqdiff.DriftField(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: Module

Hybrid drift field:

f(x, t) = β · score_θ(x, t) + residual_θ(x, t) + v_prior(x, t)

Both networks use FiLM time-conditioning. Output layer optionally has spectral normalization for Jacobian stability.

Parameters:

  • cfg (DriftConfig)

  • X_ref ((N, D) reference cell positions for velocity prior.)

  • V_ref ((N, D) velocity vectors (RNA velocity or pseudotime gradient).)

forward(x, t)[source]#
Parameters:

  • x (torch.Tensor)

  • t (torch.Tensor)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

jacobian(x, t)[source]#

Full Jacobian ∂f/∂x via autograd. Shape: (B, D, D).

Parameters:

  • x (torch.Tensor)

  • t (torch.Tensor)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

jacobian_approx(x, t, n_proj=64)[source]#

Approximate Jacobian via random projections. Shape: (B, n_proj, D).

Parameters:

  • x (torch.Tensor)

  • t (torch.Tensor)

  • n_proj (int)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

vel: KNNVelocity | None#
class scqdiff.SchrodingerBridgeConfig(dim: 'int' = 64, hidden: 'int' = 256, depth: 'int' = 4, beta: 'float' = 0.1, epsilon: 'float' = 0.1, sinkhorn_max_iter: 'int' = 100, sinkhorn_tol: 'float' = 1e-06, lr: 'float' = 0.0003, n_score_steps: 'int' = 500, convergence_tol: 'float' = 0.001, patience: 'int' = 3, max_iterations: 'int' = 50)[source]#

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • dim (int)

  • hidden (int)

  • depth (int)

  • beta (float)

  • epsilon (float)

  • sinkhorn_max_iter (int)

  • sinkhorn_tol (float)

  • lr (float)

  • n_score_steps (int)

  • convergence_tol (float)

  • patience (int)

  • max_iterations (int)

beta: float = 0.1#
convergence_tol: float = 0.001#
depth: int = 4#
dim: int = 64#
epsilon: float = 0.1#
hidden: int = 256#
lr: float = 0.0003#
max_iterations: int = 50#
n_score_steps: int = 500#
patience: int = 3#
sinkhorn_max_iter: int = 100#
sinkhorn_tol: float = 1e-06#
class scqdiff.SchrodingerBridge(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: Module

Schrödinger Bridge between source distribution ρ₀ and target ρ₁.

Parameters:

  • cfg (SchrodingerBridgeConfig)

  • X_0 ((N0, D) Source samples (e.g., young cells).)

  • X_1 ((N1, D) Target samples (e.g., old cells).)

backward_integrate(x1, steps=100, stochastic=False)[source]#

Simulate rejuvenation: ρ₁ → ρ₀.

Parameters:

  • x1 (torch.Tensor)

  • steps (int)

  • stochastic (bool)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

forward_integrate(x0, steps=100, stochastic=False)[source]#

Simulate aging: ρ₀ → ρ₁.

Parameters:

  • x0 (torch.Tensor)

  • steps (int)

  • stochastic (bool)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

jacobian(x, t, forward=True)[source]#

Full Jacobian ∂f/∂x for forward or backward drift. Shape (B, D, D).

Parameters:

  • x (torch.Tensor)

  • t (torch.Tensor)

  • forward (bool)

Return type:

torch.Tensor

train_bridge(n_iterations=None, batch_size=512, verbose=True)[source]#

Iterative Schrödinger Bridge training with convergence criterion.

Parameters:

  • n_iterations (int, optional) – Maximum iterations. Defaults to cfg.max_iterations. Training may stop earlier if the OT cost converges.

  • batch_size (int) – Pairs sampled per iteration.

  • verbose (bool) – Print per-iteration progress.

Returns:

history – ‘ot_costs’ — OT cost per iteration ‘forward_losses’ — forward score loss per iteration ‘backward_losses’ — backward score loss per iteration ‘converged’ — bool: True if early-stop triggered ‘n_iters’ — actual number of iterations run

Return type:

dict with keys:

previous

Plotting: pl

next
